content: The 2012 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded to Robert J. Lefkowitz and Brian K. Kobilka for their work on G-protein-coupled receptors. They discovered the receptors and explained how they function, which has led to a greater understanding of many diseases and the development of new treatments.
